primary_catalyst.description: Broad risk-off Friday: WTI crude broke above $90/bbl (week's 35% gain â€” biggest weekly oil move since futures trading began in 1983) as Iran war concerns kept Strait of Hormuz risk premium elevated. S&P -1.33%, Nasdaq -1.59%. Unexpected soft February NFP print (release morning of March 6) compounded inflation-plus-stagflation anxiety. NVDA dragged with Nasdaq in broader AI complex selloff.

secondary_catalysts.description: BlackRock capped withdrawals from private credit fund (first time) â€” amplified risk-off tone with credit-stress echo
Marvell +11% on AI-driven Q4 results â€” rotational pressure as diversification trade pulls flows away from NVDA even within AI theme

force_attributions.rationale: Oil shock + Iran risk + stagflation tape = cross-asset risk regime deterioration was the dominant driver on the day
Rotation into Marvell and other AI-adjacent names pulling flows out of NVDA (AI-diversification trade pattern visible all Q1)
NFP surprise + oil shock reduced likelihood of near-term Fed cuts; rate repricing pressured long-duration tech

notes: Confluence day: Iran-oil-rates-NFP all landed same session. Not a single-catalyst event.
